scopes.docs: Reference documentation, conventions, rules (`.claude/docs/` agent-facing or `docs/` user-facing)

current_goal: **v3 — Final polish before cut.** Addressing important features (memory, context management, bash output compression), architectural decisions (packaging, distribution, sync profiles), and polishing rough edges (test suite performance, frontmatter normalization). P99 tasks are deferred — ignore unless explicitly prompted. The line between "what ships in v3" and "what comes after" is being drawn now.

tasks.title: Config-driven hook parameters — read settings.json directly, document the convention
tasks.notes: Surfaced 2026-04-28 during web ecosystem exploration. Reshaped 2026-04-29 after `output/claude-toolkit/analysis/20260429_1012__analyze-idea__hooks-config-driven.md`: pattern is already half-built (`detection-registry.json` + schema + validator + perf test as the canonical model; `CLAUDE_TOOLKIT_*` env vars cover scalars). Two concrete duplications drive this task: (1) `auto-mode-shared-steps.sh` lines 92-120 hardcode the gh-write subcommand list mirroring `permissions.ask` in `settings.json` — no validator policing drift (highest priority); (2) `approve-safe-commands.sh` SAFE_PREFIXES (lines 49-92) mirror `permissions.allow` Bash entries, currently policed by `validate-safe-commands-sync.sh` — that validator becomes deletable indirection once the hook reads settings.json directly. Scope: (a) wire `auto-mode-shared-steps.sh` to read `permissions.ask` from `settings.json` at startup; (b) wire `approve-safe-commands.sh` to read `permissions.allow`; (c) delete `validate-safe-commands-sync.sh` after (b) lands; (d) extend `.claude/docs/relevant-toolkit-hooks_config.md` with explicit guidance — JSON registry for record lists (cite `detection-registry.json`), env vars for scalars (cite the `CLAUDE_TOOLKIT_*` registry), `settings.json` for permission-derived data; (e) add config-driven checklist to `/create-hook` skill. Cheapness contract: load via jq once at hook startup, match via pure-bash regex (mirrors `detection-registry.sh`). Stop-event quality gate split out to `hooks-stop-quality-gate` (P99).

tasks.title: Evaluate persistent memory systems for cross-session context
tasks.notes: Surfaced 2026-04-28 during web ecosystem exploration. Two systems to evaluate together: (1) **claude-mem** (github.com/thedotmack/claude-mem, 69k stars, AGPL-3.0) — captures tool observations via 5 lifecycle hooks, compresses context with AI summaries, retrieves via semantic search (Chroma vector DB + SQLite FTS5). Worker service on port 37777, 3-layer progressive retrieval (search → timeline → get_observations, ~10x token savings), MCP tools, web viewer UI, `<private>` tags. v12.4.8. (2) **agentmemory** (github.com/rohitg00/agentmemory, 2k stars, TypeScript) — 12 auto-capture hooks, hybrid BM25+vector search (95.2% retrieval on LongMemEval-S benchmark), 92% fewer tokens vs full-context pasting, extends Karpathy's LLM Wiki pattern with confidence scoring and knowledge graphs. The toolkit already has file-based auto-memory (`.claude/memories/`) and claude-sessions tracks session data in SQLite. Evaluate: (1) overlap/complementarity with what we have; (2) license implications (AGPL for claude-mem); (3) dependency footprint; (4) extractable patterns (progressive retrieval, observation compression, hybrid search) vs adopting full systems; (5) relationship to LSP evaluation (`eval-lsp-plugin-setup`) — both address context retrieval from different angles.

tasks.title: Bash output compression hook — token savings via smart output filtering
tasks.notes: Surfaced 2026-04-28 during web ecosystem exploration (REPOZY/superpowers-optimized). PreToolUse hook intercepts Bash calls, classifies via regex, compresses output before it enters context. Two tiers: tier 1 (near-lossless: git add/commit/push/pull/clone/fetch, npm/pip/cargo install → one-line summaries) and tier 2 (smart filtering: git status hint lines removed, git log truncated at 30 lines, passing tests → summary only, successful builds → summary, lint output grouped by severity, large ls/find truncated at 50/60 entries). Safety rails: never compresses diffs, file reads (cat/head/tail), piped commands, --verbose/--debug, curl/wget, failed commands (non-zero exit), or output under 200 chars. Adaptive re-run detection: same command within 60s passes through raw (handles "compressed output was insufficient"). Fail-open: any hook error → original command runs unmodified. Architecture: PreToolUse rewrites compressible commands to run through an optimizer script that executes + compresses; non-compressible passes through unchanged. ~76% token savings claimed on mixed sessions. Full source reviewed — well-engineered, zero-deps Node.js. Tasks: (1) evaluate feasibility as a toolkit hook (bash vs Node.js implementation, interaction with existing grouped-bash-guard); (2) decide tier scope — which compressions are worth the complexity for our workflow; (3) prototype and measure actual token savings in real sessions. Exploration entry: `output/claude-toolkit/exploration/BACKLOG.md`.

tasks.title: Evaluate syncing the CLI to raiz dist
tasks.notes: Evaluate whether and how to include `claude-toolkit` CLI in the raiz distribution profile. Open questions: (1) which subcommands make sense downstream — full CLI or a reduced subset (e.g. `backlog`, `docs`, `lessons search`, `validate` but not `sync`, `eval`, `send`); (2) install story — pip dependency, vendored script, or something else; (3) README / getting-started updates for raiz consumers who'd now have CLI access; (4) whether the CLI needs a consumer-vs-workshop mode flag or if subsetting at dist time is enough. Also consider the manifest model difference: base uses exclude-based (everything ships unless excluded), raiz uses explicit (only declared resources ship). CLI inclusion means declaring it explicitly in the raiz MANIFEST — decide granularity (whole `cli/` tree vs cherry-picked subcommands) and whether the explicit manifest model naturally handles the subsetting or needs a new pattern. Output: analysis doc with recommendation, then implementation if greenlit.

tasks.title: Declarative sync profiles — projects choose which resource categories to receive
tasks.notes: Surfaced 2026-04-28 during web ecosystem exploration. Base dist currently syncs ~34 skills to every project regardless of relevance — schema-smith gets `design-docker`, `design-aws`, `brainstorm-idea`, etc. Each unused skill still appears in the session-start skills list, consuming context tokens for zero value. VILA-Lab's "context as scarce resource" principle and Crosley's migration story (400 lines of always-loaded rules → contextual skills → 40% context reduction) both validate: don't ship what won't be used. Proposal: projects declare what they want via a manifest file (e.g. `.claude/sync-profile.json` or similar) listing resource categories or individual resources to include/exclude. `claude-toolkit sync` reads the profile and only copies matching resources. Default behavior (no profile file) = current behavior (sync everything). Categories could be coarse (`skills`, `hooks`, `agents`, `docs`) or domain-based (`core`, `frontend`, `aws`, `database`, `workflow`). Start with a design pass to decide granularity — category-based is simpler but domain-based maps better to actual usage patterns. Related to but distinct from `eval-cli-raiz-dist` (which is about whether the CLI itself ships to raiz, not about what resources ship to base).

tasks.title: Study targeted patterns from REPOZY/superpowers-optimized
tasks.notes: Surfaced 2026-04-28 during web ecosystem exploration. Production fork of obra/superpowers (already in curated resources) — 24 skills, 10 hooks, research-backed design. Not adopting the system, but three patterns warrant deeper study (bash compression split to its own task `bash-output-compression-hook`): (1) **Self-consistency reasoning** — embedded technique (not standalone) in debugging + verification skills. Generates N independent reasoning paths (3-7 depending on complexity), majority vote, acts based on agreement level (≤50% → stop and report ambiguity). Based on Wang et al. ICLR 2023. Evaluate whether this pattern improves our `code-debugger` and `goal-verifier` agents. (2) **AGENTbench findings on instruction volume** — paper "Evaluating AGENTS.md" (138 tasks) found LLM-generated context files decreased success by 2-3% while increasing costs 20%. Explore implications for how we generate and evaluate toolkit resources. (3) **"Do LLMs Benefit from Their Own Words?"** — removing prior assistant responses maintained quality while reducing context 5-10x. Models over-condition on their own output. Explore implications for session continuity and subagent prompt design. (4) **"Compliance ≠ competence" as resource-generation lens** — the finding that more instructions can actively hurt is worth evaluating as a heuristic/checklist item in `create-skill`, `create-agent`, `create-docs`, and `evaluate-*` skills: "will adding this instruction actually improve outcomes, or just increase context load?" Exploration entry: `output/claude-toolkit/exploration/BACKLOG.md`.

tasks.title: Revisit test suite runner — timing, performance, and output reliability
tasks.notes: Surfaced 2026-04-28 during `log-bash-commands` implementation. Symptom: `?s` timing + `cat: ... No such file` errors in run-all.sh / run-hook-tests.sh aggregation. Original race-condition framing disproven (xargs -P blocks before the aggregation loop reads log files). Real cause is mid-run child kill (SIGINT/SIGKILL) leaving the .log file uncreated. Status: symptom guarded in fix/test-runner-missing-log-guard (existence check + clear "(no log captured — process likely killed mid-run)" message). Root cause of the kill itself remains open — needs evidence next time it flakes. Originally also bundled inner-loop UX ideas: (a) make test-fast — rejected (slow files are the critical ones), (b) durations in summary — done, (c) test subset filter — partial (run-all.sh <substring> works but undiscoverable; no make shortcut). Flake observed 2026-04-29 during phase-1 work on hooks-config-driven: test-backlog-query.sh failed expect_output "filters by P1" on a 113-test run (got 2 P1 tasks listed in output where the test expected substring match against the first one), then passed cleanly on the immediate re-run. Hypothesis: expect_output substring-match against multi-line stdout is brittle when the fixture has multiple matching tasks and output ordering varies under parallel scheduling — the assertion finds the second task's row first and the substring isn't literally first-line. Worth investigating expect_output semantics + adding a run-many-times harness to surface other latent flakes.

tasks.title: Add prose cleanup resource to `write-documentation` + fix `draft-pr` verbosity
tasks.notes: Surfaced 2026-04-28 during web ecosystem exploration. Two parts: (1) **write-documentation prose resource** — add a kill/replace reference file (based on Clairvoyance humanizer patterns) covering: significance inflation ("powerful" → what it does), sycophantic openers (delete, start with answer), hedge stacking (one qualifier max), empty transitions (delete "Furthermore"/"Moreover"), list addiction (only for sequential steps or equal-weight items), corporate buzzwords ("leverage"→"use"), em-dash overuse (one per paragraph max, zero better), filler phrases ("in order to"→"to"), passive voice (use only when actor unknown), enthusiasm inflation (tone matches content). Concrete before/after examples for docs, commits, PR descriptions. Fits as a `resources/` file in the skill, not inline in SKILL.md. Connects to `convention-refinements-anbeeld` regularity tripwire. (2) **draft-pr vibe fix** — current output is off-vibe: sections feel over-constructed and verbose. Audit the skill's PR template and tone instructions, tighten to match the prose cleanup resource. The commit/PR examples from the humanizer are directly applicable: "Refactor auth: extract token validation" not "This PR introduces a comprehensive suite of improvements."

tasks.title: Frontmatter normalization + token size validator
tasks.notes: Promoted P99→P2 2026-04-28 — token size validator adds concrete value beyond polish. Two parts: (1) **Frontmatter field ordering** — `build-communication-style` uses non-standard order (`name, description, argument-hint, allowed-tools, type`); most skills use `name, type, description, ...`. The A1 sweep resolves `type:` placement but doesn't normalize broader ordering. Could be automated with a small linter or sed pass. (2) **Token size validator** — validation script that flags oversized resources (skills, agents) that get listed in the system prompt. The Agent Skills spec (agentskills.io) recommends <5000 tokens for SKILL.md body; name+description (~100 tokens) loads at startup via progressive disclosure. A script that measures and warns on bloat would catch it before shipping. Integrate into `make validate` or as a standalone `claude-toolkit validate` check.

tasks.title: `surface-docs.sh` hook — context-aware doc surfacing
tasks.notes: Promoted P99→P2 2026-04-26 — direct follow-up to `session-start-output-too-large`. The cap fix shrinks `essential-conventions-{code_style,execution}` to Quick Reference (§1) + path nudge; this hook extends the same surfacing pattern to `relevant-*` docs, matching tool context against doc keywords and injecting a one-liner ("relevant doc available: `.claude/docs/<name>.md` — Read on demand") when a relevant doc hasn't been loaded. Reuses the §1-extraction primitive landed in the cap fix. Same deterministic algorithm as `surface-lessons.sh` — intra-session dedup (v2.63.6) + 2+ keyword-hit threshold (v2.63.7). Original P99 demotion rationale ("observe surface-lessons for a few weeks before replicating") is moot: capacity pressure from the 10KB cap forces the issue earlier than the design-validation timeline anticipated, and the cap fix itself validates the §1-surfacing pattern. Coordinates with `.claude/hooks/` queue item 5.

tasks.title: Revisit toolkit documentation for consumers — CLI help, guided introduction
tasks.notes: Surfaced 2026-04-26. The toolkit ships ~34 skills, 7 agents, 14 hooks, 17 docs to consumer projects via `claude-toolkit sync`, but a downstream user landing on a freshly-synced project has no clear entry point — `README.md` and `docs/` are the only signals, and `claude-toolkit --help` is currently a thin wrapper over its argparse subcommands. Two distinct gaps to address: (1) **CLI help quality** — `claude-toolkit <subcommand> --help` is mostly auto-generated argparse text; could land harder with examples per subcommand and a top-level `claude-toolkit help` that organizes subcommands by purpose (sync resources, query backlog/docs/lessons, send suggestions, version checks). (2) **Guided introduction** — possibly a `/welcome-toolkit` or `/tour-toolkit` skill that walks a downstream user through "here are the resources you got, here's how skills/agents/hooks differ, here's how to discover what's available, here's how `claude-toolkit sync` works, here's how to send feedback." Audience is a Claude Code user opening a project that already has the toolkit synced, not someone setting up the workshop. Open questions: skill vs doc vs both; whether the tour reads `docs/indexes/` directly or has its own narrative; how it stays fresh as resources are added. Inputs: `cli/` module (current help text), `docs/getting-started.md` if it exists, `README.md`, the existing `setup-toolkit` skill (which is workshop-side, not consumer-side). Output: brainstorm doc proposing scope + shape; implementation likely staged.

tasks.title: Evaluate independence of lessons ecosystem from analytics ecosystem
tasks.notes: Demoted P2→P3 2026-04-28. Surfaced 2026-04-26 during the projects-text-id alignment (v2.68.2). The lessons CLI and hooks now defer to `~/.claude/sessions.db` for canonical project_id resolution — a hard cross-ecosystem dependency: `_detect_project()` in `cli/lessons/db.py` errors when sessions.db exists but the encoded dir isn't in `project_paths`, and `_resolve_project_id` in `.claude/hooks/lib/hook-utils.sh` warns + leaves PROJECT empty in the same case. This was the right call to prevent name drift, but it raises a broader question: should the lessons ecosystem (capture + surface + manage rules across projects) work without the analytics ecosystem (sessions.db, hooks.db, project_paths, the indexer)? Today the lessons DB schema is owned by claude-sessions (`schemas/lessons.yaml`), the projects dimension uses claude-sessions' resolution chain (override / git_remote / regex_fallback), and the basename fallback only fires when sessions.db is *entirely absent*. Standalone-toolkit users (no claude-sessions installed) work today; partial-install users (sessions.db present but stale or empty) hit the strict error. Scope of evaluation: (1) catalog every cross-ecosystem coupling — schema ownership, projects table, FK directions, env var conventions (`CLAUDE_ANALYTICS_*`), backup-script ownership (already a P3 item: `move-backup-lessons-to-claude-sessions`); (2) identify which couplings are essential (data correctness — name drift) vs incidental (operational — backup script lives in toolkit but DB is sessions-owned); (3) decide whether lessons should ship a self-contained mode (own its projects dimension, no sessions.db dependency) or whether the current "claude-sessions is required when present" stance is the desired end state. Inputs: the projects-text-id design context (this CHANGELOG 2.68.2), the `move-backup-lessons-to-claude-sessions` P3 task (related question of where utilities live), `.claude/docs/relevant-toolkit-lessons.md` (current ecosystem reference). Output: a brainstorm/analysis doc in `output/claude-toolkit/`, possibly leading to a roadmap of either "decouple" or "formalize the dependency" tasks.

tasks.title: Convention refinements from Anbeeld/AGENTS.md patterns
tasks.notes: Promoted P99→P3 2026-04-28 — patterns resurfaced during VILA-Lab review. Surfaced 2026-04-28 during web ecosystem exploration (github.com/Anbeeld/AGENTS.md, 59 stars; github.com/Anbeeld/WRITING.md, 33 stars). Three patterns worth extracting — not adopting their files, just informing our own conventions: (1) **Explicit priority stack** — "correctness > evidence > safety > minimal changes > consistency > performance" as a codified tiebreaker. We have these priorities implicitly but no single ranked list. Could land in `essential-conventions-code_style` or a new lightweight convention doc. (2) **Evidence proportionality** — graduated rule: trivial edit → inspect target file; behavioral/API/dependency change → trace execution path, call sites, constraints, regression surface before editing. Currently lives only in agent-level prompts, not as a global convention. (3) **Regularity tripwire for `write-documentation`** — self-check rule: "name the most repeated pattern; if it appears 3+ times or dominates two consecutive paragraphs, rewrite one." Fits as a required-check in the `write-documentation` skill, not a global convention. Apply when next touching these docs/skills, not as standalone work.

tasks.title: Add structured reasoning activation to select agents
tasks.notes: Promoted P99→P3 2026-04-28 — connects to `study-superpowers-optimized-patterns` (P1) which evaluates self-consistency reasoning. Some agents would benefit from explicit reasoning technique activation (CoT, hypothesis-evidence patterns, structured decomposition). `code-debugger` already does this organically with its hypothesis-elimination approach. Audit other agents — candidates: `code-reviewer` (risk assessment reasoning), `goal-verifier` (backward verification logic), `proposal-reviewer` (audience perspective reasoning). Light touch — add reasoning prompts where they'd improve output, not a framework overhaul.

tasks.title: `/design-aws` skill — idea to deployable AWS architecture
tasks.notes: Reference + satellite ready; user-postponed (no dependency blockers). Phased workflow: understand idea → design architecture (output: structured markdown doc) → generate diagram via `/design-diagram` with AWS icons → translate to aws-toolkit input configs (YAML) → review (security-first, then architecture). Leverages aws-toolkit for deterministic generation. Also depends on aws-toolkit v1 input format stability. When skill ships: enforce satellite-contract rule — link out to aws-toolkit docs via CLI convention (see `satellite-cli-docs-convention` task), no duplicated spec in workshop. Design doc: `output/claude-toolkit/design/20260329_1517__brainstorm-idea__design-aws.md`. Drafts: `output/claude-toolkit/drafts/archive/aws-toolkit/` — pre-research on IAM validation tools, cost estimation tools, service selection.

tasks.title: Boundary coverage for `test-suggest-json.sh`
tasks.notes: 5 assertions for a 95-line hook (`suggest-read-json.sh`); missing size-threshold boundary tests (just-under, exactly-at, just-over). Correctness gap (off-by-one in size policy), not a security gap — separated from the P0 settings.json work because it's a different threat class. Add ~3 boundary assertions exercising the threshold value defined in the hook source. Surfaced 2026-04-26 in `output/claude-toolkit/analysis/20260426_1710__design-tests__expect-test-value-audit.md` (Finding 5).

tasks.title: Re-evaluate `suggestions-box/` as satellite convention + `claude-toolkit send --to`
tasks.notes: Today only the workshop (claude-toolkit) has `suggestions-box/` and a documented triage workflow. Satellites (claude-sessions, aws-toolkit, schema-smith, validation-framework) don't — when sending a note from claude-toolkit *to* claude-sessions on 2026-04-26 (the harness-attachment-types observations), I had to `mkdir -p` the destination ad-hoc. Two related changes to evaluate together: (a) add an optional `--to <project-path>` flag to `claude-toolkit send` so you can write to *another* project's `suggestions-box/` without `cd` — current behavior writes to *this* project's box, which is the receive direction only; (b) standardize `suggestions-box/` (with a tiny CLAUDE.md pointing at the workshop's full triage workflow rather than duplicating it) as part of satellite scaffolding. Deferred to evaluate after the pattern gets organic use — if `--to` lands and gets exercised across satellites, the convention case strengthens; if cross-satellite traffic stays at one note a quarter, the formal convention is overkill. Surfaced 2026-04-26 during the SessionStart cap fix wrap-up.

tasks.title: Remove ecosystems opt-in session-start nudge
tasks.notes: After `ecosystems-opt-in` ships, session-start shows a one-time nudge to projects that predate the new schema (no `CLAUDE_TOOLKIT_LESSONS` / `CLAUDE_TOOLKIT_TRACEABILITY` env keys in settings.json). The nudge is self-extinguishing per-project (setup-toolkit writes the keys → nudge stops firing), but the code itself should be deleted once all user projects have been updated. Triggered manually rather than version-based because toolkit ships faster than the user reaches each project. Signal to remove: user says "remove the opt-in nudge" or equivalent. Delete the relevant section from `.claude/hooks/session-start.sh` and any related tests.

tasks.title: Fix `md5sum` for macOS
tasks.notes: macOS ships `md5` (BSD) and `shasum`, not `md5sum`. Hits: `cli/eval/query.sh:44`, `tests/test-evaluation-query.sh:61`. Both suppress errors with `2>/dev/null`, so on macOS every resource gets an empty hash; every resource appears stale on `claude-toolkit eval stale`. Fix: switch to `shasum -a 256` or guard with `command -v md5sum`. Deferred until eval core ships. `mktemp --suffix` portion done in v2.72.10.

tasks.title: Update eval scripts `2>/dev/null` for macOS compatibility
tasks.notes: `cli/eval/query.sh` (lines 44, 117, 131, 162, 230), `tests/test-evaluation-query.sh:61`, and `tests/lib/test-helpers.sh:61` suppress errors with `2>/dev/null` on `md5sum` and `jq` calls. The `md5sum` sites overlap with `macos-md5sum`. The jq sites follow the same dangerous pattern fixed in validators (v2.72.11). Deferred because eval isn't relevant for macOS consumers yet. Analysis: `output/claude-toolkit/analysis/20260427_1700__analyze-idea__macos-loud-errors.md`.

tasks.title: v3 E3 — `teardown-worktree` artifact-copy scope decision
tasks.notes: Currently copies only `output/claude-toolkit/reviews/*` from worktree to parent at teardown. Does not copy `pr-descriptions/`, `design/`, `plans/`, `sessions/`. Decide: (a) deliberate — keep per-worktree ephemera scoped, only review artifacts persist; or (b) broaden to include other `output/claude-toolkit/` subdirs a user is likely to want after teardown. No clear right answer; needs a decision before implementing.

tasks.title: Add interactive option selection to skills that ask questions
tasks.notes: AskUserQuestion supports single-select, multi-select, and preview panes — but most skills default to open-ended questions. Audit skills that use AskUserQuestion (brainstorm-idea may already use options organically) and convert categorical decision points to structured option selection where it fits. Keep free-text for creative/descriptive input.

tasks.title: Explore resource-aware model routing for agent spawning
tasks.notes: Currently agents hardcode `model: "opus"` or `model: "sonnet"`. Some tasks (simple evaluations, pattern searches, file lookups) could route to Haiku for cost/speed without quality loss. Explore: (1) which agents/tasks are candidates for cheaper models, (2) whether this should be a convention in create-agent or a runtime decision by the spawning skill, (3) what the actual cost/quality tradeoff looks like in practice. Start with a discussion pass, not implementation.

tasks.title: Add `claude-toolkit lessons demote` subcommand
tasks.notes: Demoted P3→P99 2026-04-26 — workaround (raw SQL one-liner) is documented and the motivating sweep didn't need it. Surfaced 2026-04-26 during the Key-tier crystallization sweep. CLI exposes `promote`/`deactivate`/`absorb`/`crystallize` but no inverse of `promote`. The Key Promotion Contract in `manage-lessons/SKILL.md` §4.5 lists "demote back to recent" as one of three valid paths when revisiting a Key lesson, but the workaround is currently raw SQL: `sqlite3 ~/.claude/lessons.db "UPDATE lessons SET tier='recent' WHERE id='<ID>';"`. Add a thin subcommand mirroring `promote`'s shape (single `--id`, sets tier='recent', no other side effects). Add when convenient. Design context: `output/claude-toolkit/design/20260426_1623__design__key-lessons-review.md`.

tasks.title: Add macOS CI runner
tasks.notes: Demoted P1→P99 2026-04-27 — all code fixes landed and validated by a real macOS consumer; new bash scripts are infrequent enough that CI regression catching doesn't justify the maintenance. GitHub Actions `macos-latest` + `make check`. Revisit if macOS-specific regressions recur.

tasks.title: Per-project customization of detection registry
tasks.notes: Demoted P3→P99 2026-04-26 — speculative pre-design with no user yet (note already says "defer until v1 ships and we have real-world signal"). Follow-up to `hooks-detection-target-convention`. Once the shared detection registry (`detection-registry.json` + JSON Schema + jq loader) is in place and its schema has stabilized, add a layered resolution path so downstream projects can extend or override the toolkit defaults without forking the synced file. Likely shape: project-local `detection-registry.local.json` (gitignored or committed per project preference) merged on top of the synced toolkit registry at hook-load time. Open questions to resolve at design time: (1) merge strategy — replace-by-id, append-only, or deep-merge per field; (2) whether projects can *disable* a toolkit-shipped entry (e.g. `{"id": "github-pat", "disabled": true}` override) or only add new ones; (3) whether the local file is project-private or syncs out via `claude-toolkit send` for cross-project sharing; (4) precedence rules when both files define the same `id` with different `kind`/`target`. Defer until v1 ships and we have real-world signal on which downstream projects need custom patterns (likely: aws-toolkit for AWS-specific shapes, schema-smith for DB-specific shapes). Design context: `output/claude-toolkit/brainstorm/20260425_1349__brainstorm-feature__hooks-detection-target-convention.md`.

tasks.title: Fold `surface-lessons.sh` into `grouped-bash-guard.sh`
tasks.notes: Demoted P3→P99 2026-04-26 — pure perf (~40ms avg savings) on a baseline that may already be lower after dedup work. Re-measure first before any implementation. `surface-lessons.sh` currently averages ~106ms with ~30-40ms of that being bash+jq startup overhead. Fold the Bash branch into `grouped-bash-guard.sh` to skip a second process spawn; keep Read/Write/Edit path separate or extend `grouped-read-guard.sh` to cover Write|Edit. Constraints: async-injection contract (PreToolUse additionalContext), 5s timeout, current matcher is `Bash|Read|Write|Edit` (wider than grouped-read's `Read`). P2 in `output/claude-toolkit/analysis/20260423_2309__analyze-idea__improve-lessons-lifecycle.md`; the 106ms baseline predates dedup + 2-hit threshold and may already be lower.

tasks.title: `review-security` — worthyness diagnostic
tasks.notes: Demoted P3→P99 2026-04-26 — skill has never been invoked, which is already the answer in spirit. Run invocation-frequency check (same approach as pattern-finder agents diagnostic). Based on data: (a) Keep — content already solid; (b) Sharpen — broaden description triggers and/or add surfacing-hook path; (c) Deprecate — CC's built-in /security-review may cover enough of the surface. Do alongside pattern-finder diagnostic for consistency.

tasks.title: Rename `.claude/docs/` to `.claude/conventions/`
tasks.notes: Demoted P3→P99 2026-04-26 — note's own framing is "schedule when nothing else is touching those paths", which is a P99 timing constraint even though the rename has architectural value. `.claude/docs/` is overloaded — name suggests user-facing docs but contents are agent-loaded conventions/rules (`essential-*`, `relevant-*`, `codebase-explorer/`). Shared name with top-level `docs/` hides the audience split (agent context vs user-facing). "rules" conflates with Claude Code's native rules concept, so `conventions/` is the preferred name. Coordinated rename: (1) move files, (2) update session-start loader and surface-* hooks, (3) update sync paths in CLI + dist profiles (base and raiz MANIFESTs), (4) update CLAUDE.md "Structure" section, (5) update `claude-toolkit docs` command and any skill references (grep for `.claude/docs/`), (6) update downstream satellites' synced copies via next sync. Non-trivial churn.

tasks.title: Evaluate LSP plugin setup for the toolkit ecosystem
tasks.notes: Surfaced 2026-04-28 during web ecosystem exploration. Claude Code has native LSP client support (confirmed in v2.1.121 `--bare` flag docs) with 14 LSP plugins in the official marketplace (`anthropics/claude-plugins-official`). Plugins are thin JSON manifests — e.g. pyright-lsp just declares `{"command": "pyright-langserver", "args": ["--stdio"], "extensionToLanguage": {".py": "python"}}`. Tasks: (1) install `pyright-lsp` plugin, test impact on code navigation speed/accuracy for this bash+python codebase; (2) assess whether custom LSP configs (without the marketplace plugin) are viable — the plugin format is trivial enough to self-author; (3) evaluate what LSP capabilities are actually exposed (blog claims goToDefinition/findReferences/hover — verify against real behavior); (4) decide whether LSP belongs in the toolkit's recommended setup or stays opt-in per-project. Skip Serena (tried before, too much bloat) and typescript-lsp (not needed). Cross-reference with `eval-claude-mem` for broader context-retrieval strategy. Source: karanbansal.in/blog/claude-code-lsp/ (mixed real+fabricated details — use official plugin docs as canonical source).

tasks.title: `backlog render` ignores `--priority` filter (writes to literal `--priority` file)
tasks.notes: Surfaced 2026-04-29: `claude-toolkit backlog render --priority P1,P2,P3` should filter the rendered BACKLOG.md output, but instead treats `--priority` as the output path argument and writes the rendered backlog into a literal file named `--priority` in the cwd. Also reports "Rendered 30 tasks to --priority" — count is the full set, ignoring the intended filter. Root cause likely in cli/backlog/render.sh argument parsing — render does not accept the `--priority` flag, but the dispatcher passes it through as a positional path. Either reject unknown flags or implement priority filtering for render.

tasks.title: Stop-event quality gate hook — auto-lint resource changes at response finalization
tasks.notes: Split out 2026-04-29 from `hooks-config-driven` (was bundled as a Stop hook idea). Inspired by Crosley's blog-quality-gate (12-module linter on modified content at response finalization). We have no Stop hooks today — quality checks are all manual via evaluate-* skills. A lightweight Stop hook could catch oversized skills, broken frontmatter, or prose antipatterns automatically. Connects to `v3-e5-frontmatter-ordering` (token size validator — directly consumable by a Stop gate) and `prose-cleanup-resource` (12-module linter equivalent for prose). Should consume from the config-driven hooks convention once that convention is documented (`hooks-config-driven`). Open questions before promoting: which checks fire (resource files only? all writes?), block-vs-warn semantics, perf budget on Stop event, scope per-resource-type vs global.

tasks.title: Validator for ERE-metachar entries in settings.json permissions
tasks.notes: Surfaced 2026-04-29 by goal-verifier on hooks-config-driven (review at output/claude-toolkit/reviews/20260429_1217__goal-verifier__feat-hooks-config-driven.md). The lib/settings-permissions.sh loader audits each Bash() prefix for ERE metacharacters and silently skips entries that contain `*?+()[]{}^$\|` (with a stderr warning that no one will see in production). Today's permissions.{allow,ask} are clean, but a future contributor adding e.g. `Bash(npm run|test:*)` or `Bash(node --inspect=*:*)` would see the entry vanish from the hook's effective list with no make-check signal. Add a validate-settings-permissions.sh that runs in make validate: walk both buckets, flag entries that would be silently dropped by the loader. Cheap (single jq + a regex audit). Not a current defect — strictly preventative.

tasks.title: Periodically audit read-only verb allowlist in block-config-edits.sh
tasks.notes: Fix #4 introduced a path-side default-deny for .claude/settings.json: any write-shaped verb alongside the path blocks. Read-only verbs (cat, grep, jq, head, tail, wc, diff, ls, find, stat, file) on the path pass through. As new read-only tools (bat, rg, eza, etc.) become common, false-positive blocks would surface when reading settings — extend the allowlist when that happens. Living as a recurring backlog task because the allowlist isn't enumerated in code (the invariant is documented inline; comment-only persistence won't materialize as a reminder).

tasks.title: Move settings-integrity state file out of repo to ~/.claude/
tasks.notes: The integrity check stores its baseline at .claude/logs/settings-integrity.json — in-repo, gitignored, but LLM-writable. A session can rewrite settings.json then update the state file so the next session sees no drift (silent). Move state to $HOME/.claude/settings-integrity/<project-id>.json (per-machine, outside the repo's hook surface) so tampering requires escaping the project sandbox. Closes the cheapest bypass after committing the rewrite.

tasks.title: Settings-integrity: verify covering-commit author + remote presence
tasks.notes: Current integrity check trusts any commit covering the change as evidence of legitimate edit. An LLM that runs git commit on a settings.json rewrite silences the warning. Tighten by (a) verifying the covering commit's author matches git config user.email/user.name, and (b) checking the commit exists on origin (not just local). Both signals make a programmatic commit harder to forge. Trade-off: noisier on legitimate uncommitted-but-author-matched edits — needs a UX call.

tasks.title: block-config-edits: catch write-through-symlink when match_ doesn't fire
tasks.notes: Fix #4 added a symlink defense (creation + write-through), gated by match_config_edits firing. The match_ predicate fires on settings-shaped paths or a narrow set of write verbs (>, >>, tee, cp, mv) — it does NOT fire on a bare 'echo > /tmp/link' when the link was planted out-of-band. The symlink-creation step is still blocked, so the bypass requires the link to exist before the LLM session — different threat model. Documented gap; revisit if it becomes reachable. Either broaden match_ further (cost: more check_ invocations) or accept and document.
